hypothesis: The systematic underestimation of band gaps in MOFs containing 3d transition metals by GFN1-xTB can be compensated by training a metal-specific δ-learning correction model that incorporates electronic structure descriptors (such as d-orbital occupancy and spin multiplicity) rather than relying solely on stoichiometric and structural features, thereby enabling accurate high-fidelity band gap predictions for transition metal MOFs with a single unified model.
